challenges.title: 2.1 Signal Buffers

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Elias, look at these data streams. They feel... familiar.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: We should use NumPy to organize these raw signal values into an array.

challenges.defaultCode:
import numpy as np
raw_signals = [0.1, 0.4, 0.9, 0.2]

# Convert raw_signals into a numpy array called 'buffer'

challenges.title: 2.2 Vital Vectorization

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: The sensor calibration is off. Every reading is -0.5 below actual.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: We can fix all of them at once using broadcasting. Add 0.5 to the 'vitals' array.

challenges.defaultCode:
import numpy as np
vitals = np.array([36.2, 35.8, 36.5])

# Add 0.5 to vitals without a loop and save to 'calibrated'

challenges.title: 2.3 Buffer Reshaping

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: This memory dump is a flat list, but it's supposed to be a 2x3 grid.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Can we reshape the 'flat_log' into a 2D array called 'grid'?

challenges.defaultCode:
import numpy as np
flat_log = np.array([1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1])

# Reshape into (2, 3)

challenges.title: 2.4 The Memory Log

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: We found a structural log. It's stored in CSV format.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Let's load this string into a Pandas DataFrame called 'df'.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
import io
csv_data = 'id,temp\n1,22\n2,25\n3,19'

# Load using pd.read_csv(io.StringIO(csv_data))

challenges.title: 2.5 Null Anomalies

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Elias, some memory fragments are missing. We see 'NaN' everywhere.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Use df.fillna(0) to repair the corrupted entries.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.DataFrame({'val': [10, np.nan, 30]})

# Fill missing values with 0

challenges.title: 2.6 Hazardous Purge

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: These corrupted sectors are beyond repair. We must purge them.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Use df.dropna() to remove any row with a missing value.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.DataFrame({'id': [1, 2, 3], 'status': ['ok', np.nan, 'ok']})

# Drop rows with NaN

challenges.title: 2.7 Sector Query

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: The archive is huge. We only need the 'Critical' alerts.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Filter 'df' for rows where level == 'Critical'.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
data = {'id': [1,2,3], 'level': ['Low', 'Critical', 'Low']}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Filter and save to 'critical_df'

challenges.title: 2.8 Cargo Sorting

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: We need to find the average weight of cargo in each sector.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Group the 'df' by 'sector' and calculate the mean 'weight'.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
data = {'sector': ['A', 'A', 'B'], 'weight': [100, 200, 50]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Use groupby('sector').mean()

challenges.title: 2.9 Integrity Audit

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: We need a quick summary of the whole archive's integrity scores.

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Use the .describe() method on 'df' to generate statistics.

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({'integrity': [88, 92, 45, 12, 99]})

# Generate stats summary
stats = None

challenges.title: Boss: Labyrinth Collapse

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Vanguard is here! The archives are collapsing!

dialogueIntro.name: Diana
dialogueIntro.text: Clean the data and find rooms with integrity > 80. Hurry!

challenges.defaultCode:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
data = {'id': [1, 2, 3, 4], 'integrity': [85, np.nan, 92, 40]}
df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# 2. Filter for integrity > 80
